hook: A good invite feels specific enough to trust and light enough to decline.
body: Clarity lowers friction, while low pressure protects dignity for both people.

hook: Vague pseudo-plans keep you in limbo because neither person knows who is actually supposed to lead.
body: A narrow plan is kinder than an open-ended maybe, even when the answer is no.

hook: A narrow plan is kinder than an open-ended maybe, even when the answer is no.
body: Naming a day, neighborhood, and rough format usually performs better than asking if she wants to hang sometime.

body: Send one plan with clear details and no apology language, then let the answer teach you something.
